query: I've been working on this feature all week with Claude helping me debug CORS issues and refactor the auth module. Before I merge, I want to document what happened — what prompts I used, what worked, what didn't. Can you generate some kind of summary?
show me what I did with Claude this sprint. We had 3 major PRs and I want to share the AI collaboration patterns with my team lead in our 1:1
/www-insights --from 2w
generate a weekly insights report of my Claude Code sessions so I can paste it into our Notion sprint retro page
I need to write my weekly status update and I want to include how I used AI this week. what sessions did I have and what were the key decisions?
이번 주 Claude로 작업한 내역 정리해서 슬랙에 올릴 수 있게 만들어줘
I want to track how my AI usage is evolving over the past month — am I getting better at prompting? what patterns work?
give me an HTML report of all my Claude sessions from last week, with charts showing tool usage and a breakdown of which prompts led to the best outcomes
we just finished the sprint and I want to create a retrospective doc that shows how the team used AI — total sessions, key decisions made with Claude, and friction points
commit this with a record of what AI helped me do since my last commit, including the key prompts I used
before I push this PR, can you attach a summary of the AI collaboration to the commit message? I want the team to see what prompts drove the key decisions
www-commit
can you read the file at /Users/me/project/src/auth.ts and explain what it does?
fix the TypeScript compilation error in my project, the build is failing on line 42 of utils.ts
write unit tests for the UserService class using jest and mock the database connection
I need to set up a new Next.js project with Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui components. Can you scaffold it?
review this pull request and check for security vulnerabilities, especially around the SQL queries in the data layer
help me debug why my Docker container keeps restarting — here's the docker-compose.yml and the logs show OOM killed
refactor this 500-line function into smaller modules following clean architecture principles
